properties.name: Alamo Recreation Center Teaching Garden
properties.description:
ARCTG is a community-driven space dedicated to teaching sustainable gardening practices and promoting food security. This garden offers hands-on workshops, volunteer opportunities, and educational programs for people of all ages.
Learn More

properties.name: Patterson Park Community Garden (PPCG)
properties.description:
PPCG is dedicated to making community gardening accessible. Gardeners keep or share the food they grow, while the community fosters collaboration, support, and responsible land stewardship. Those interested can join the waitlist for a plot.
Learn More

properties.name: UT Farmstand
properties.description:
Student-run program at the University of Texas at Austin that provides affordable, fresh produce and locally sourced groceries to the campus community. All food items are sold at discounted rates in Jester Plaza every other Wednesday.
Learn More
properties.Eligibility: Open to UT students, faculty, and staff only
properties.Hours: Wednesdays, Feb. 2, Feb. 16, Mar. 9, and Mar. 23 from 11 a.m. – 4 p.m.
properties.Comments: Markets occur every other Wednesday from 11 a.m. - 3 p.m. The last market was on April 16th.

properties.name: UT Microfarm
properties.description:
Student-run, organic urban farm. Includes a managed farm and community garden with rental plots. Volunteer workdays combine hands-on farming with education on food issues. Farm produce is donated to UT Outpost.
Learn More
properties.Eligibility: Open to UT students, faculty, and staff only

_umap_options.name: Community Gardens
_umap_options.description: Community gardens offer a hands-on way to supplement groceries with fresh fruits and vegetables. They empower individuals to take control of their food access, learn about sustainable growing practices, and reduce grocery costs.
